highlights: 1. Support SST Treatment for Rental and Leasing Services
A new SST tax code SVR-6 has been introduced to support 6% Service Tax for Rental or Leasing Services, with 2% service tax exemption based on the latest SST treatment.
This enhancement ensures correct invoice display, SST reporting, and e-Invoice submission mapping.
1.1 Added New Tax Code SVR-6
A new tax code SVR-6 has been introduced in Tax Code Maintenance > Configure Malaysia SST > Output Tax.
1.2 Invoice Display Enhancement
When using the SVR-6 tax code, the invoice will continue to display 6% SST at both the invoice line level and invoice summary.
System will automatically calculate the 2% exempted service tax amount and make it available as a new field that can be displayed on the invoice.

features: Improve Send Email security in detecting spam
Enhance File Upload validation to reject if file type is not supported
fixes: Fix recurring template incorrect “Next Date” if edit start date to another date
Fix incorrect costing calculation on cost with many decimals
Fix incorrect stock card balance for zero quantity
Fix FreshChat (Livechat) send message will have error when phone number is invalid

features: Enhanced Cash Book and Journal to capture Business Name and Business Registration Number when a Tax Entity is selected.
Improved email sending security.
Improved the company blocked error message.
fixes: Fixed an error when generating Consolidated e-Invoice where having many transactions to be consolidated caused the description to be too long and resulted in an error.
Fixed an error when generating Singapore InvoiceNow Aggregated Transactions where having many transactions to be aggregated caused the description to be too long and resulted in an error.
Fixed an issue where the SST 02 Report format did not display the SST number in the designated field.
highlights: Here are the enhancements for v1.1.78

1. Enhanced Cash Book and Journal – Tax Entity Information Cash Book Entries and Journal Entry have been enhanced to automatically capture the Business Name and Business Registration Number when a Tax Entity is selected, ensuring more complete and accurate records.
2. Improved Email Sending Security Enhanced the security of the Send Document by Email and Send Statement function to ensure safer and more reliable email delivery.

3. Improved Company Blocked Error Message Updated error message from "This Company is blocked." error message to "This company is blocked. Please contact your sales representative for assistance or email us at sales@autocountsoft.com "。This message is prompt when the users did not continue subscribe to Cloud Accounting service on that company for more than 1 years.
These enhancements were made available on 14 Jan 2025!

features: Added Singapore InvoiceNow function.
Updated the Reset SST Processor hint text to remove the mention of SST on Payment, which is not supported.
Enhanced validation when deleting a Debtor or Customer to check if it is being used in OneSales.
fixes: Fixed an issue where files could not be downloaded via Live Chat.
highlights: Here is the enhancements for v1.1.77
1. Singapore InvoiceNow function.

Added Singapore InvoiceNow function to support electronic invoice exchange via the Peppol network. This enables businesses to send and receive e-invoices in compliance with Singapore’s nationwide e-invoicing initiative.
These enhancements were made available on 19 December 2025!

features: Added new setting: “Allow duplicate barcodes”
Restricted date filter for expired subscriptions for Stock Card & Stock Balance Report
AI Agent support added for Live Chat Support
highlights: Here is the enhancements for v1.1.76 .!
1. Added new setting: “Allow duplicate barcodes”
A new option has been introduced to control whether duplicate barcodes are allowed. By default, the setting is unticked.
2. Restricted date filter for expired subscriptions for Stock Card & Stock Balance Report
When an account book’s subscription has expired, users can only filter dates up to the subscription end date plus 2 extra days .
Example: If the subscription ends on 31 Oct 2025 , the maximum filterable date will be 3 Nov 2025 .
3. AI Agent support added for Live Chat Support
AI Agent is now available to help in Live Chat Support. Don’t worry — if it can’t solve your issue, you can still chat with our Live Agent.

features: Added new tax codes and update latest SST-02 form changes
Improved Knock Off Entry screen to display more rows in the knock-off section on smaller screens.
fixes: Fixed incorrect decimal format for Unit Price in Self-Billed reports.
Fixed issue where importing Debtor and Creditor opening balances failed due to trailing spaces
Fixed header alignment issues in Financial Reports when resizing from Letter to A4 during printing.
Fixed issue where copying a Self-Billed document with Tax Setting disabled did not auto-assign “Not Applicable” to the Tax Type field, resulting in submission errors.
highlights: We hope the enhancements in v1.1.75 make it easier for you to stay compliant with Malaysia’s latest regulations!
1. Added new tax codes and update latest SST-02 form changes
Section B2 - Service Tax Details
Item 11:
The total tax value previously combined for 6% and 8% in row c) is now split into:
c) Total Tax Value @ 6%
d) Total Tax Value @ 8%
The previous 11 d) item has been pushed to 11 e) due to this change

highlights: We hope the enhancements in v1.1.74 help you work more smoothly and productively.
1. Added new access right options for the Accounting Role in User Maintenance

For companies that require stricter control over Cash Book entries, this can now be managed through the Accounting Role in User Maintenance. Two new checkboxes have been introduced — “Not allowed to access Receipt Voucher” and “Not allowed to access Payment Voucher.”

Based on these access rights, Cloud Accounting has been enhanced to display Receipt Voucher and Payment Voucher functions and records accordingly in Cash Book Entry, Print Cash Book Entry Listing, and related modules such as Recurring Payment Voucher and Import Cash Book Entry. These improvements ensure that user access permissions are consistently enforced across all relevant functions.
2. Added Bank Reconciliation Report under Bank Transactions.

A new Bank Reconciliation Report has been added under the Bank Transactions module, allowing users to easily review and verify transactions recorded in the system against actual bank statements. This report helps ensure accuracy, identify discrepancies, and streamline the reconciliation process.
3. Enhanced Self-Billed e-Invoice to allow copying from multiple transactions into one e-Invoice document
The Self-Billed e-Invoice feature now supports copying from multiple transactions, allowing you to create a consolidated self-billed e-Invoice in one go.

features: Added new function to support automatically email validated e-Invoice for Malaysia e-Invoice.
Added tooltip and link to Knowledge Base Portal for help articles.
[SG] Enhanced Singapore GST Instant Info to capture realized gain/loss in “Total Value of Exempted Supplies” (Box 3).
Enhanced Automatic Issue Date Time setting to avoid prompting users ‘e-Invoice issuance date time is too old’ message
Enhanced Stock Costing calculation to 4 decimal places to improve precision.
Supported Consolidated e-Invoice to include rounding in the calculation.
fixes: Fixed Import Journal Entry not balance error caused by source data containing decimal values that were not visible in Excel.
Fixed Import Journal Entry and Cash Book Entry error where “Can’t remove account [AccountNo]” appeared.
Fixed Import Tax Entity from Excel issue where the ‘City’ field was automatically truncated to 10 characters.
highlights: We hope the updates in v1.1.73 help you work more seamlessly and effectively.
1. Added new function to support automatically email validated e-Invoice for Malaysia e-Invoice.
AutoCount makes e-Invoicing easier for you. Once validated, your e-Invoice and credit note will be automatically sent to your customers.
2. Added tooltip and link to Knowledge Base Portal for help articles
To make navigating in Cloud Accounting easier, we’ve added tooltips (the ? Icon) that provide quick guidance and direct access to related help articles.
3. [SG] Enhanced Singapore GST Instant Info to capture realized gain/loss in “Total Value of Exempted Supplies” (Box 3)
Previously, this information was only available after running the GST Processor, but now it can be viewed directly in Instant Info.
4. Enhanced Automatic Issue Date Time setting to avoid prompting users ‘e-Invoice issuance date time is too old’ message

features: Added hint text message when selecting ‘Individual’, ‘Foreign Business’, or ‘Government’
Enhanced the Tax Entity page with ‘Local Business’ and ‘Foreign Business’ radio selections
Added a new filter option, “Hide Item Description in Sales/Purchase Transactions,” to the Ledger report
fixes: Fixed report header values will not be updated when editing the Report Header and clicking Set Default Report Header in the Company Profile
highlights: We valued your feedback and here are some of the updates from v1.1.72!
1. Added hint text message when selecting ‘Individual’, ‘Foreign Business’, or ‘Government’
To enhance user-friendliness, we have included a message when selecting ‘Individual’, ‘Foreign Business’, or ‘Government’. If the TIN field is empty, the general TIN will be used.
2. Enhanced the interface on the Tax Entity page with ‘Local Business’ and ‘Foreign Business’ radio selections.
Upon selecting ‘Business’ under Classification, two additional options ‘Local Business’ and ‘Foreign Business’will appear next to it for further selection.
3. Added a new filter option, “Hide Item Description in Sales/Purchase Transactions,” to the Ledger report

To enhance flexibility, you can now have an option to hide the Item Description in ledger report. This is useful if you wish to have a summarized ledger on your sales instead of showing each line from the invoice.
These enhancements were made available on 11 August 2025!
1. Adjusted to make Tax Exemption Number entry not mandatory when using tax codes EEM, ESP, and EDA for Malaysia e-Invoice documents.
You no longer need to enter the Tax Exemption No. under ‘Document Info’ when using tax codes EEM, ESP, or EDA for e-Invoice submission!
2. [SG] Optimize Singapore GST Instant Info page to load data more fluently
These enhancements were made available on 31 July 2025!

features: Support Automatic ‘Issue Date Time’ setting for Malaysia e-Invoice
Support Default Classification Code for Malaysia e-Invoice
Enhanced to allow editing Tax Entity field in Customer and Supplier
Enhanced Malaysia e-Invoice’s Annexure fields to match with LHDN latest field length size
Improved user experience when using Upload QR, Search TIN, and Validate TIN, even without selecting a Tax Entity in the Company Profile.
fixes: Fixed error where Cash Book was able to generate recurring transactions even when the Payment Method, Tax Code, or Department was set to inactive
Fixed error where editing Tax Entity could not change TIN field to empty (leaving it blank) TIN for foreign businesses
Fixed error where the Consolidated e-Invoice detail drill down information to trace source documents are matched incorrectly
highlights: We valued your feedback and here are some of the updates from v1.1.70!
1. Support Automatic ‘Issue Date Time’ setting for Malaysia e-Invoice

But first, what is ‘Issue Date Time’? On LHDN MyInvois Portal, taxpayers are only allowed to submit e-Invoices no earlier than 3 days before the invoice’s document date. This function is designed to handle backdated invoices where the document date is more than 3 days before the current date, and the Invoice has not yet been submitted to LHDN for validation. Example:
New  Automatic ‘Issue Date Time’ setting : Previously, you had to set this date manually. Now, with the Automatic ‘Issue Date Time’ setting:
2. Support Default Classification Code for Malaysia e-Invoice

This feature automatically applies the selected default classification code to all new line items during document entry. It is especially useful when most of your products fall under the same classification, eliminating the need to assign the classification code to each product individually in ‘Product’ maintenance.
3. Enhanced to allow editing Tax Entity field in Customer and Supplier
We understand mistakes can happen when mapping Tax Entities to customers or suppliers, we have now enhanced to allow edit so you can switch to the correct Tax Entity easily.

Added new function – Upload Taxpayer's QR Code
Enhanced to not allow duplicate Supplier Invoice Number in Purchase Invoice on the same supplier

highlights: 1. Added new function – Search TIN at Tax Entity

I’m sure you’ll appreciate this new addition — the Search TIN feature is directly integrated with LHDN via API. It allows suppliers to easily obtain a customer’s TIN using their Business Registration Number (for businesses) or Identity Card Number (for MyKAD holders). You can access this feature under ‘Tax Entity’ in Master Data .
Please note: Only the TIN will be provided by LHDN. Other fields such as address, state, etc., will need to be filled in manually.
2. Added new function – Upload  Taxpayer's QR Code
Say goodbye to manual input — simply ask your customer to send their QR code from the MyInvois Portal. All you need to do is upload it here, and their Tax Entity information will be auto populated.
3. Enhanced to not allow duplicate Supplier Invoice Number in Purchase Invoice for the same supplier

This enhancement helps prevent duplicate entry of the same supplier invoice in Purchase Invoice. An error prompt will appear upon saving if the same supplier invoice number is entered for the same supplier.
4. Added Save Layout function in Master Data and Product Inquiry

features: Support Batch actions in Sales, Purchase, Accounting and Stock Documents
fixes: Fixed incorrect validation “Payment amount must be less than or equal to total amount” when the invoice was partially knocked off and then edited to key deposit amount
Fixed an issue with auto generating customer or supplier code after hitting 1000 codes
highlights: v1.1.68 is here!
1. Support Batch actions in Sales, Purchase, Accounting and Stock Documents

To enhance the user experience, we've introduced batch functions that allow you to perform actions in bulk — including batch approval (Invoices and Credit Notes), batch submission for approval, batch email sending, batch deletion, and batch voiding.
Batch void, send email and delete
Batch submit for approval and approve
The new release is available on 2 June 2025. That’s it about it today!

features: Added a new “Invoice with Payment” system report
highlights: A useful enhancement for you in v1.1.67!
1. Added a new “Invoice with Payment” system report
We understand that you would like the outstanding balance of your invoice to be displayed after a customer makes a partial payment, so they are aware of the outstanding amount due. Yes, here it is!

Note, it will be able to detect the amount received keyed in at invoice entry ‘Deposit Payment Amount’ and at Cash Book Entry’s Receipt. [ Different between ‘Payment Amount in invoice entry and Cash Book Entry ]
Example of how this report works:

On 23/5/2025, you issued an invoice for RM1,000 to Flower Sdn Bhd. On the same day, Flower Sdn Bhd made a partial payment of RM100. On 26/5/2025, they made another payment of RM200, leaving an outstanding balance of RM700.
Step 1: You create an invoice amounted to RM1000
Step 2: Then at invoice entry, you keyed in ‘RM100’ under ‘Deposit Payment Amount’

highlights: v1.1.66 is here!
1. Displays a short phrase indicating that status-filtered results are results for the last 365 days.
We’ve added a short phrase to indicate that when filtering by status (e.g., Full Payment, Partial Payment, Pending, etc.), only transactions from the last 365 days are shown.
The new releases are available on 20 May 2025. That’s it about it today!

features: Import from Excel – Limit row size for better performance
Import from Excel – Error shown for wrong Excel date format
Optimize some performance issue with Knock Off Entry form when there are too many documents available for knock off
highlights: 1. Import from Excel – Limit row size for better performance
To ensure a smooth import process, we have optimized the import as outlined below.
1.1. Transactions import (Quotation, Invoice, Purchase Order, Cash Book Entries, etc)

- Each import is limited to 300 records. Please note that this count refers to master records only—for example, count invoice row (master records), not invoice detail row, so you can upload up to 300 invoice rows.
1.2. Master Data import (Customer, Supplier, Product, etc) - Each import is limited to 1000 records.
If the limit is exceeded, an error message will be displayed after selecting the Excel file, and the data will not be loaded into the preview.
2. Import from Excel – Error shown for wrong Excel date format Now it prompts the correct error message when the Date provided in the Excel column does not match with the date format chosen.
The new releases are available on 9 April 2025. That’s it about it today!

highlights: 1. Enhance to display Supplier Document Number in Purchase Invoice and Purchase Return Audit Trail Listing Report

For convenience, we have added ‘Supplier Invoice No.’ to the report template of the Purchase Invoice and Purchase Return Audit Trail Listing. If a Supplier Invoice No. is entered in a Purchase Invoice transaction, it will be displayed in both the Purchase Invoice and Purchase Return Audit Trail.
Preview Purchase Invoice
Preview Purchase Return Audit Trail Listing
The new releases are available on 25 February 2025. That’s it about it today!

highlights: 1. Added option “Show Supplier Doc. No” to show supplier document number In Creditor statement

With this option ‘Show Supplier Doc. No’ in Creditor Statement, now your suppliers can easily do cross-checking against their own original document (sales invoices) if you provide them with your statement. You can enable this option at the Print Report window!
Please note Supplier Doc. No is being captured in ‘Supplier Invoice No.’ field in Purchase Invoice.
2. Added new filter option ‘Document Status in ‘Print Quotation Listing’ report

Need a report to show how many quotations are Success, Pending, or Loss? Yes, with this new filter option, you can easily generate a report based on filter options, All, Pending, Success, Lost and Closed.
3. Enhanced Import Chart of Account Excel Template to include information about SpecialAccType at ‘Guide’ tab

The newly added table will definitely be helpful in guiding you when importing the Chart of Account! Please remember to always check the informational Guide tab when using the import function in AutoCount Cloud Accounting!
4. Enhanced to prompt warning if there is another Paid Plan for similar company name

features: File attachment function in transactions
Enhance Recurring Template to support last day of each month
Enhance Recurring Template to support filling Date format at Description to capture billing date and year
fixes: Fix incorrect Next Date in Recurring Template
highlights: 1. File attachment function in transactions

The upload attachment function is now available in Invoice, Purchase Invoice, Receipt Voucher, and Payment Voucher. This function allows you to store your source digital copy (PDF, Word, Excel etc) together with your transactions so they can be easily tracked. Each individual file attachment must not exceed 10MB .
2. Enhance Recurring Template to support last day of each month

We have added ‘last day’ selection so that if you want to set the recurrence to be on last day of the month, 30 th or 31 st , you can now do so. Example, if you set last day, the invoice generated in June will 30 th , while July will be 31 st .
3. Enhance Recurring Template to support filling Date format at Description to capture billing date and year

Additional enhancements have been made to Recurring Transactions. If you are creating a recurring payment voucher for monthly rental and want to include the date in the description, this is now possible. This function allows the description to be updated automatically without manual editing.
The new releases are available on 11 January 2025.That’s it about it today!

highlights: 1. Added Recurring function

You can now set up instruction to recur your Invoice, Journal Entry and Payment voucher. You can set it up at their respective modules. The recurring transaction function in AutoCount Cloud Accounting automatically generates transactions for you once the recurring instructions are set up. Say goodbye to manually creating transactions for every billing cycle!
2. Access Right control for Bank Balance, Document Summary and Journal Entry for Accounting Role
If you do not want your employee to view Journal Entry, now you can do so in our enhanced User Setting.
3. Access Right control for Dashboard and Document Summary for Accounting, Sales and Purchase Role
Dashboard and Document Summary might be sensitive information to your employees, you can also turn it off now under User Setting.
4. Support Other Country – United Kingdom

If you have United Kingdom company with home currency Great British Pound (GBP), AutoCount Cloud Accounting can support your business too.     5. Improve similar name detection for Product, Customer and Supplier

features: Added Singapore Seamless e-Filling
Enhance Consolidated e-Invoice for OneSales POS transaction to group no tax with zero tax details together

highlights: In the release note of v1.1.60, we have added a new function for Singapore and some enhancement when posting from OneSales POS to Cloud Accounting !
1. Singapore Seamless e-Filling

Now our Singapore users can enjoy Seamless e-Filing in Cloud Accounting, upon clicking on the ‘Seamless e-Filing’, you will be redirected to a new tab to start performing your e-filing. You are able to submit and download your Financial Statement (in simplified XBRL format) and your Annual return to ACRA. In addition, you are able to submit your Form CS to IRAS as well as download the detailed P&L and tax computation generated.
2. Enhance Consolidated e-Invoice for OneSales POS transaction to group no tax and zero tax details together
The new release are available on 18 October 2024. That's it about it today !

highlights: In the release note of v1.1.59, we have added THREE new Import templates – just for you, as well as some minor enhancement and fixes. Let’s see what we have for you!
1. Import Chart of Account, Cash Book Entry and Journal Entry

The Import templates will help to ease the process of migration from another accounting system or Word/Excel to AutoCount Cloud Accounting. The new templates are Chart of Account,  Cash Book Entry (Receipt Voucher & Payment Voucher) and Journal Entry . You easily download the templates in our system. There is always a ‘Guide’ tab in the Excel to let you know how to import.

highlights: In the release note of v.1.1.58, there are some minor fixes and also few enhancements on the e-Invoice module to make everything better. Remember we also have a series of guide file on e-Invoice here .
Now let's us have a look at the new enhancements together !
1. Not showing watermark "Draft" for Delivery Order
We have enhanced to not show the "Draft" watermark when preview or printing your invoice using "Delivery Order" format.
2. Document Inquiry in e-Invoice Module
Under Document Inquiry, we had added two new functions 'Submission Summary’ and ‘Submitted Documents’ to keep track of e-Invoice submission records.

Submission Summary : A summary to show the numbers of documents that are valid, invalid, in progress, cancelled, consolidated and to be consolidate. This will give you an idea of how many e-invoices are valid, and most importantly, pending to be consolidated!
Submitted Documents:  Shows you a list of submitted documents with details such as Document number, date, total, e-Invoice status etc.
